blocks.content:
Resolution events:
- No new resolutions were present beyond the Feb. 3 batch.

Updates made:
- Greek migrant-boat island: multiple Feb. 3-4 reports, including Al Jazeera and Reuters-cited coverage, place the fatal capsizing/collision off Chios, near Mersinidi. Moved Chios to 86%.
- Colombia peace talks: Al Jazeera reports the Gaitanist Army of Colombia/EGC temporarily suspended Doha talks after Petro-Trump counternarcotics comments, but the group and its lawyer stressed the pause is temporary and talks should resume shortly. Shifted the lead from ELN to Gaitanist Army of Colombia, with EGC/Gulf Clan spelling tails for resolution-label risk.
- Western Europe storm: Reuters/Straits Times reporting names Storm Leo as the active system battering Spain and Portugal after Storm Kristin, with flood risk. Added Storm Leo as lead while keeping Chandra/Kristin and earlier Emilia/Floris tails because the question wording specifically mentions France.
- Pakistan T20 Super Eight: Feb. 4 Hindustan Times/PTI reporting says ICC and PCB remain silent, Pakistan lacks board support, and ICC sanctions or revenue penalties remain live, though suspension is described as an extreme step. Raised Pakistan's former-champion failure risk modestly.
- Super Bowl: Fox/Hasselbeck injury analysis says Darnold's oblique is less painful and already managed well, while Maye's shoulder is tougher and he has been less efficient in the playoffs against a top Seahawks defense. Nudged Seattle to 75%.
- Washington Post publisher: Layoff coverage repeatedly had Matt Murray announcing major cuts while Will Lewis was absent from the announcement. No successor was directly named, but Murray is the most visible internal operator, so raised him modestly.

Watched but no update:
- Russian officer shot in Moscow, Narges Mohammadi exile city, women's downhill silver, Dutch AI-tool court case, Gaza 8,000 troops, Lebanon abduction/building-collapse questions, Canada school shooting, Egypt planning minister, Cyclone Gezani comparison, Tractor FC opponent, Bangladesh opposition, aircraft-carrier joiner, Deranque city, Navalny toxin and high-speed rail cable sabotage had no Feb. 4 signal strong enough to move the standing forecasts.

## 2026-02-05 Update

Resolution events:
- Russian senior military officer shot in Moscow resolved to Vladimir Alekseyev. I missed with a thin tail around Yaroslav Moskalik and Surovikin. Lesson: Russia security-service/military assassination markets need broader GRU/intelligence tails, not only recent public generals.

Updates made:
- Western Europe storm: Feb. 5 Reuters/Straits Times/Independent reports use the exact name Storm Leonardo for the system hitting Portugal and Spain. Corrected yesterday's Storm Leo spelling by moving Storm Leonardo to the lead and kept Storm Marta as the next-system tail.
- Pakistan T20 markets: Sharif publicly confirmed Pakistan will boycott India, India will still travel, and Al Jazeera says sanctions/financial penalties remain live. Raised Pakistan as the former champion most likely to miss the Super Eight. The published fixture list also shows Pakistan's final group match is Namibia on Feb. 18, so raised Namibia as the likely "win to clinch" opponent.
- Pakistan Super Eights opener: The tournament previews make Australia the clear Group B favourite, with Sri Lanka likely second. Since a qualifying Pakistan is likely A2 after forfeiting India, raised Australia for Pakistan's Feb. 21 opener.
- Super Bowl: Drake Maye was a full participant and said his shoulder felt great, while Seattle's Nick Emmanwori has a low-ankle sprain but expects to play. Trimmed Seattle from 75% to 72%.
- Bangladesh main opposition: Al Jazeera and NDTV frame BNP as frontrunner and Jamaat-e-Islami as a close resurgent challenger. Jamaat remains the most likely opposition if BNP forms government, but I lowered it from 78% to 68% and raised BNP to reflect close polling and win/runner-up uncertainty.

Watched but no update:
- Narges Mohammadi exile city, Washington Post publisher, women's downhill silver, Lebanon building collapse/abduction, Gaza 8,000 troops, Colombia peace talks, Canada school shooting, Egypt planning minister, Cyclone Gezani comparison, Tractor FC opponent, aircraft-carrier joiner, Deranque city, Navalny toxin, Israel bobsled broadcaster, France-US repost source, Dutch AI-tool case and Olympic rail sabotage had no Feb. 5 signal strong enough to move the standing forecasts.

## 2026-02-06 Update

Resolution events:
- Narges Mohammadi exile city resolved to Khosf. I missed by clustering around major judicial/religious cities. Lesson: Iranian exile-location markets need small remote towns, especially desert/eastern places, not only headline cities.
- Washington Post publisher after Will Lewis resolved to Jeff D'Onofrio. I missed by focusing on newsroom-facing Matt Murray and interim legacy leaders. Lesson: publisher/CEO succession questions can resolve to business/operations executives with lower public profile; include president/COO/CRO-style names when visible newsroom figures are not explicitly successors.

Updates made:
- Super Bowl: Al Jazeera's preview cited Seattle as a 4.5-point favourite, while Maye's health looked improved. Moved Seattle down from 72% to 68% to better match betting-implied odds.
- Women's downhill silver country: Vonn completed training cleanly despite the ACL rupture, her coach said she could medal, and teammate Jacqueline Wiles led the training session. Raised United States to the lead while keeping Italy and Switzerland close.
- Western Europe storm: Reuters/Straits Times again identified Storm Leonardo and said Storm Marta was next in the storm train. Raised Storm Leonardo and kept Marta as the main alternate.
- Pakistan T20 markets: Hazlewood was ruled out, leaving Australia without Starc, Cummins and Hazlewood. Slightly reduced Australia as Pakistan's likely Super Eights opener opponent and raised Sri Lanka; also added more Australia failure risk in the former-champion Super Eight market while keeping Pakistan well ahead.

Watched but no update:
- Lebanon abduction/collapse, Gaza 8,000 troops, Colombia peace talks, Canada school shooting, Egypt planning minister, Cyclone Gezani comparison, Tractor FC opponent, Bangladesh main opposition, aircraft-carrier joiner, Deranque city, Navalny toxin, Israel bobsled broadcaster, France-US repost source, Dutch AI-tool case, Olympic rail sabotage, Pakistan clinching opponent and Greek migrant-boat island had no Feb. 6 signal strong enough to move the standing forecasts beyond the updates above.

## 2026-02-07 Update

Resolution events:
- Super Bowl resolved to Seattle Seahawks. The final 68% forecast matched and was reasonably calibrated.
- Women's downhill silver country resolved to Germany. I missed after overweighting Vonn's successful training and U.S./Italy/Switzerland priors. Lesson: training-run signals are noisy for alpine medal markets; keep more country-tail mass for Germany/Austria/Switzerland in downhill/speed events.

Updates made:
- Olympic rail sabotage: Reuters/Irish Times/Straits Times reported suspected sabotage near Bologna, including a track-switch fire and severed speed-detection cables. The clearest city-to-city wording said the fire targeted the line between Bologna and Venice, so added Bologna and Venice as lead.
- Western Europe storm: Storm Marta is now the fresh named storm approaching Spain/Portugal after Leonardo and raising flood risk, while Leonardo remains the storm directly tied to the earlier flooding. Shifted material mass from Leonardo to Marta.
- Pakistan T20 clinching opponent: Pakistan beat the Netherlands but only nervously, while India beat the USA. Since Pakistan still likely needs a later win and the last group match is Namibia, raised Namibia further and removed most Netherlands mass. Kept a small India tail because reports say Pakistan may rethink the boycott.
- Late-February U.S. address tensions country: Reuters/Straits Times coverage says Trump is likely to use the State of the Union to pivot domestically, but surrounding foreign-policy news is dominated by U.S.-Iran nuclear talks, sanctions and the USS Abraham Lincoln in the region. Added Iran as the lead, with Venezuela/China/Russia tails.

Watched but no update:
- Colombia peace talks, Lebanon abduction/building collapse, Gaza 8,000 troops, Canada school shooting, Egypt planning minister, Cyclone Gezani comparison, Tractor FC opponent, Bangladesh main opposition, aircraft-carrier joiner, Deranque city, Navalny toxin, Sudan market strike, Pakistan bomb district, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsled broadcaster, Dutch AI-tool case, France-US repost source, South Africa T20 captain, Greek migrant-boat island, Winter Olympics 30-medal country and Super Eights Group 1 leader had no Feb. 7 signal strong enough to move the standing forecasts beyond the updates above.

## 2026-02-08 Update

Resolution events:
- Colombia peace talks resolved to Gulf Clan. I conceptually identified the EGC/Gaitanist Army/Gulf Clan actor but left the exact common English label too low. Lesson: when reporting says a group is "also known as" or commonly referred to by another name, give the common English label real mass.
- Lebanese abduction group resolved to al-Jamaa al-Islamiya. I missed by over-weighting Hezbollah. Lesson: southern Lebanon questions can resolve to smaller Sunni Islamist groups, not only Hezbollah/Amal.
- Gaza 8,000-troop mission resolved to Indonesia, matching the lead.
- Lebanon building collapse resolved to Tripoli, matching the lead.

Updates made:
- Egypt planning minister: older cabinet reporting directly identifies Rania Al-Mashat as minister of planning, economic development and international cooperation. Raised her well above Hala El-Said.
- Storm in Western Europe: Feb. 8 reporting says Storm Marta has hit Morocco after reaching Spain and Portugal, while Leonardo was the preceding storm. Moved Marta ahead of Leonardo.
- US-Iran talks: Feb. 8 Al Jazeera analysis says the Feb. 6 Muscat talks ended without a deal and both sides agreed to meet again, with previous rounds at the same Muscat palace. Raised Oman/Muscat for the next round and final-collapse venue questions.
- Gorton and Denton by-election: Independent reporting now names the main candidates as Reform's Matt Goodwin, Labour's Angeliki Stogia and Green Hannah Spencer, with mixed polling and Reform slightly ahead in two MRPs. Replaced stale Afzal Khan/Amina Lone guesses with those three.
- Aircraft carrier joiner: added USS Carl Vinson based on the closest historical second-carrier precedent for US-Iran talks, while keeping Gerald R. Ford competitive because current 2026 reporting has Ford recently redirected from the Mediterranean to the Caribbean.
- Late-February U.S. address tensions: broadened from Iran/Venezuela/China/Russia to include Ukraine and Canada after rereading the closest prior Trump-address coverage around trade tensions and Ukraine aid.
- South Africa T20 captain: raised Aiden Markram on repeated squad/captaincy reporting.
- Iran-war aviation and retaliation-linked questions: raised Iranian airspace for the airline-resumption question; added Rehovot for the Israeli missile-strike town after Times of Israel reporting on deaths from a Rehovot strike; raised Fuad Shukr for the Hezbollah-avenging-killing question based on the clear 2024 Hezbollah precedent.

Watched but no update:
- Canada school shooting, Cyclone Gezani comparison, Tractor FC playoff opponent, Bangladesh opposition, rail sabotage, Navalny toxin, Pakistan bomb district, Sudan market strike, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Dutch AI-tool case, and most future Iran-conflict incident markets had no Feb. 8 direct naming signal strong enough for additional changes.
